_metadata.title: ComplianceLint Interim Standard for EU AI Act Article 6: Classification Rules for High-Risk AI Systems
_metadata.disclaimer: This is an interim standard developed by ComplianceLint based on available guidance and the official EU AI Act text. It is NOT an official standard. It will be superseded when CEN-CENELEC publishes the harmonized standard for Article 6 (expected Q4 2026).
basis: EU AI Act Annex I (Union harmonisation legislation)
EU AI Act Annex III (High-risk AI systems)
EU AI Act Article 6(3) exception criteria
European Commission guidelines on high-risk classification (2025)
_metadata.awaiting_standard: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for Art. 6 classification (expected Q4 2026)

step_1.question: Is the AI system a safety component of a product covered by EU harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I, OR is it itself such a product?
step_1.if_yes: HIGH-RISK via Art. 6(1) — subject to third-party conformity assessment under Annex I legislation

step_2.question: Does the AI system fall under one of the use-case categories listed in Annex III?
step_2.if_no: NOT HIGH-RISK under Art. 6 — may still be subject to other AI Act provisions (e.g., Art. 52 transparency obligations for certain AI)

step_3.question: Does the Art. 6(3) exception apply? The system is NOT high-risk if it does NOT pose a significant risk of harm to health, safety, or fundamental rights AND it meets at least one of: (a) performs a narrow procedural task, (b) improves the result of a previously completed human activity, (c) detects decision-making patterns without replacing human assessment, (d) performs a preparatory task for an assessment listed in Annex III.
step_3.if_yes: NOT HIGH-RISK (Art. 6(3) exception) — provider must document this reasoning
step_3.if_no: HIGH-RISK via Art. 6(2) — subject to Chapter 2 requirements (Arts. 8-15)

annex_iii_categories.description: AI systems intended for biometric identification, categorisation, or emotion recognition
subcategories: Remote biometric identification (real-time and post, in publicly accessible spaces)
Biometric categorisation by sensitive attributes (race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious beliefs, sex life/orientation)
Emotion recognition in workplace and education

annex_iii_categories.description: AI systems intended as safety components in the management and operation of critical infrastructure
subcategories: Road traffic management
Water supply
Gas supply
Heating supply
Electricity supply
Digital infrastructure

annex_iii_categories.description: AI systems that determine access to or affect progression in education and vocational training
subcategories: Access or admission decisions
Assignment to institutions or programs
Assessment of learning outcomes (when used to determine progression)
Monitoring and detection of prohibited behaviour during exams (cheating detection)

annex_iii_categories.category: Employment, workers management and access to self-employment
annex_iii_categories.description: AI systems used in recruitment, selection, and management of workers
subcategories: Recruitment and selection (CV screening, interview evaluation)
Decisions on promotion, termination, task allocation
Monitoring and evaluation of worker performance and behaviour

annex_iii_categories.category: Access to essential private services and public services and benefits
annex_iii_categories.description: AI systems evaluating eligibility for essential services
subcategories: Creditworthiness assessment (credit scoring)
Risk assessment and pricing for life/health insurance
Evaluation of eligibility for public assistance benefits
Dispatching or priority-setting for emergency services
Risk assessment for emergency response

annex_iii_categories.description: AI systems used by law enforcement authorities
subcategories: Individual risk assessment (recidivism, reoffending)
Polygraph and similar tools
Evaluation of reliability of evidence
Predicting occurrence or reoccurrence of criminal offences (predictive policing)
Profiling of natural persons in criminal investigations
Crime analytics on large datasets

annex_iii_categories.category: Migration, asylum and border control management
annex_iii_categories.description: AI systems used in migration and border control
subcategories: Risk assessment for security, irregular migration, or health risks
Examination of applications for asylum, visa, and residence permits
Document authenticity verification
Polygraph or similar tools during examination

annex_iii_categories.category: Administration of justice and democratic processes
annex_iii_categories.description: AI systems assisting judicial authorities or used in democratic processes
subcategories: Research and interpretation of facts and law, application of law to facts
Alternative dispute resolution
Influencing the outcome of elections or referendums (excluding organizational tools)

art6_3_exception_criteria.description: An AI system listed in Annex III is NOT high-risk if it does NOT pose a significant risk AND meets at least one of these criteria:

criteria.label: Narrow procedural task
criteria.description: The system performs a narrow procedural task (e.g., converting unstructured data to structured, document classification for filing)

criteria.label: Improvement of prior human activity
criteria.description: The system improves the result of a previously completed human activity (e.g., grammar check on a human-written decision)

criteria.label: Pattern detection without replacement
criteria.description: The system detects decision-making patterns or deviations without replacing or influencing the human assessment (e.g., flagging inconsistencies for human review)

criteria.label: Preparatory task
criteria.description: The system performs a preparatory task to an assessment relevant to the use cases in Annex III (e.g., data preprocessing before a human makes the actual decision)

art6_3_exception_criteria.documentation_requirement: The provider MUST document their reasoning for invoking the Art. 6(3) exception and notify the relevant national authority BEFORE placing the system on the market.

scoring.high_risk_art6_1: System is a safety component or product under Annex I legislation — HIGH-RISK
scoring.high_risk_art6_2: System falls under Annex III and Art. 6(3) exception does NOT apply — HIGH-RISK
scoring.not_high_risk_exception: System falls under Annex III but Art. 6(3) exception applies — NOT HIGH-RISK (must document reasoning)
scoring.not_high_risk: System does not fall under Annex I products or Annex III use cases — NOT HIGH-RISK under Art. 6
scoring.unable_to_determine: Insufficient information to classify — manual review required
